cards.front: What do command arguments and options represent in a UNIX command?
cards.back: Values passed to a command to specify its behavior or parameters

cards.front: What is the purpose of the dash (-) in UNIX commands?
cards.back: Separates command arguments from command options or flags

cards.front: What type of argument can be a file name?
What is the purpose of the equals sign (=) in UNIX commands?
cards.back: Used to assign or set a value for an option or parameter

cards.front: What type of argument requires no values?
